twitter_thread: 40% of users abandon apps due to poorly designed settings. Fixing this can boost engagement by 25%.
Bevy 0.19's App Settings use Feathers Widgets to reduce options by 30%, simplifying UX.
Contact Shadows in Bevy 0.19 decrease visual noise by 20%, making settings easier to navigate.
Richer Text in Bevy 0.19 increases setting readability by 15%, reducing user frustration.
Mistake to avoid: having more than 7 settings options. 90% of users won't use them.
Bevy 0.19's Post Processing Effects enhance settings UI with a 10% increase in visual clarity.
What's the most frustrating app setting you've encountered and how would you redesign it? #appsettings #ux
pack.linkedin: Bevy 0.19's release highlights a crucial issue: 50% of apps have settings that 99% of users never touch. To fix this, use tools like Feathers Widgets to simplify settings, reducing options by 30%. For example, limiting settings to 5 key options can increase user engagement by 25%. What's your experience with app settings - have you designed any that users actually use?
tiktok_hooks: 1 in 5 users ditch apps due to bad settings
Clicking 10 times to get to settings? No thanks
pack.reddit_headline: Should apps have fewer than 5 settings options?
